Job Title: Senior Talent Growth and Leadership Partner
Ad Text:
What's the role?

In this position, you will partner closely with HR and business stakeholders to design and deliver high impact, scalable learning interventions that directly support our strategic priorities. Your responsibilities include:

Strategic Capability Planning: Designing and implementing a cohesive annual learning strategy that clarifies desired skills and proficiency levels across all employee cohorts.

Curriculum Design & Delivery(facilitation): Leading the development of innovative learning programs that translate theory into practical, on-the-job skills.

Leadership Development: Engaging with leaders to deliver specific development programs for emerging leaders and specialised workshops.

Supplier & Content Management: Selecting and managing external learning suppliers and maintaining a centralised repository learning resources.

Project Oversight: Planning and prioritising multiple development activities to meet objectives within specified timescales and budgets.

Who are you?

We are looking for a strategic thinker who can translate complex business objectives into effective learning solutions. You should possess:

Consultative Business Partnering: A proactive approach with the ability to challenge the status quo and drive constructive change.

Stakeholder Influence: Exceptional relationship-building skills and the ability to consult effectively with varied levels of leadership.

Design Expertise: Strong knowledge of adult learning principles, blended learning methodologies, and contemporary instructional design models.

Strategic Mindset: The ability to see ahead to future possibilities and prioritise initiatives for the greatest organisational impact.

Effective Communication: Excellent verbal and written skills, with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for any audience.

Commitment to Excellence: A results-driven attitude that consistently achieves goals and holds self and others accountable to high standards.

Innovative Spirit: An openness to new ideas and a drive to apply innovative methods, tools, and technology to learning.

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

This role is part of the Human Resources (HR) team where our people vision is to unlock News’ potential for growth in an ever-changing media landscape, we equip passionate people with the skills to excel, supported by an innovative and collaborative environment. Our people’s ideas and unique perspectives take centre stage, celebrated and delivered through the widest range of media brands and channels in Australia.

We’re focused on delivering the News Corp Australia strategy through delivering a future ready workforce and an innovative and dynamic workplace with automated, seamless and personalised experiences in a sustainable, high performing organisation

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. In order for us to assist you, please ensure you include the role title you are applying for in the subject line. For more information regarding Diversity at NewsCorp Australia please visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers under the Diversity@News section.

Job Title: Client Partner, Delivery Lead - Data & Technology
Ad Text:
We’ve been retained to appoint a Senior Client Partner to join a growing Sydney-based team, leading the delivery of complex programs while supporting key client relationships and growth.

This is a delivery-led role at its core. You will be responsible for overseeing and guiding programs of work end-to-end, ensuring quality outcomes, strong governance, and successful execution. Alongside delivery, you will contribute to account development and identify opportunities for expansion within existing clients.

The Role
You will play a pivotal role in leading delivery across multiple engagements, acting as the senior point of accountability for outcomes. While you will be aligned to key clients, the focus is on delivering high-quality work and building long-term trust through execution.

This is not a pure sales or account management role. Your credibility will come from your ability to lead delivery and consistently achieve results.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the delivery of complex programs across strategy and technology implementation
Take accountability for outcomes across the full delivery lifecycle
Provide oversight, structure, and governance across multiple engagements
Build strong, trusted relationships through consistent delivery excellence
Support account strategy and identify opportunities for growth within existing clients
Collaborate with sales and pursuit teams to shape opportunities where relevant

About You

You are a senior leader/manager with a strong track record of delivering programs, not just managing relationships.

Proven experience leading large-scale or complex program delivery
Background in consulting, ideally at Senior Manager or Principal level
Strong understanding of delivery frameworks, governance, and execution
Ability to engage stakeholders across both business and technology
Commercial awareness, with the ability to spot opportunities through delivery
Motivated by ownership, accountability, and delivering outcomes

Why This Role

This is an opportunity to step into a senior role where delivery is the foundation of your impact.

You’ll lead meaningful programs of work while contributing to the growth of key client relationships, without being purely sales-driven.

Join a large, ASX-listed manufacturing and packaging business with a significant national footprint, supporting some of Australia’s most recognisable brands. This organisation is known for its scale, operational complexity, and ongoing investment in commercial capability.

We are seeking an experienced Senior Financial Modeller to join on a 2–4 month contract, focused on delivering high-impact modelling and commercial insights across the business.

This role will see you partnering closely with senior stakeholders to build and refine models that drive key decision-making, particularly across operations and supply chain.

Key Responsibilities:

Build robust financial models from the ground up in Excel
Develop scenario and “what-if” modelling across labour costs, COGS and profitability
Translate financial data into clear commercial insights for non-finance stakeholders
Analyse P&L performance and provide meaningful context and recommendations
Support decision-making across operations, supply chain and leadership teams
Leverage Power BI for visualisation and insights where required
Work with SAP data to ensure accuracy and integrity of inputs
About You:
You are a highly capable and commercially minded finance professional with a strong track record in financial modelling within complex environments.
You will bring:

Proven experience in manufacturing, FMCG or similarly operationally complex industries
Advanced Excel skills, with demonstrated experience building models from scratch
Exposure to Power BI and SAP
Strong capability in scenario modelling, particularly around labour and cost drivers
The ability to interpret a P&L and provide clear, actionable commercial insights
Confidence partnering with senior stakeholders and influencing decision-making
This is an excellent opportunity to step into a fast-paced environment and deliver tangible impact over a short period.
Immediate availability or short notice preferred.

Apply:
To discuss this opportunity in more detail, please contact Danielle Bower on 0473 489 543 or dbower@sharpandcarter.com.au

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little differently. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing…

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a key role within our Commercial IT portfolio, supporting the delivery of technology initiatives that enable commercial strategy and drive business value.

This role will primarily focus on initiatives across ancillary products and services, helping to enhance the customer journey while supporting revenue growth. You will also contribute to a broader range of commercial and technology projects across the business.

You will act as the bridge between business and technology, leading end-to-end Business Analysis activities across complex initiatives, ensuring solutions are aligned to both customer and commercial outcomes.

This role is based in our Brisbane Head Office. 

You will….

Lead end-to-end stakeholder engagement and requirements gathering across small to large, complex projects
Analyze business problems, map current processes and define future-state solutions that improve efficiency and customer outcomes
Support delivery of commercial and ancillary initiatives, identifying opportunities to optimize revenue, conversion and customer experience
Develop high-quality documentation including business requirements, process maps and solution designs
Facilitate workshops, interviews and discovery sessions to align stakeholders and drive clarity
Own Business Analysis deliverables, ensuring outputs are completed on time and to a high standard
Collaborate closely with Project Managers, Solution Architects and delivery teams to support planning, solution design and testing
Maintain requirements traceability and ensure delivered solutions meet business needs
Translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders
Build strong relationships across Technology and the broader business, acting as a trusted advisor
Identify opportunities to streamline processes, reduce duplication and support continuous improvement

You will be great in this role if you have…

5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst, ideally across complex, fast-paced environments
Strong experience leading stakeholder engagement and end-to-end requirements gathering
Demonstrated commercial acumen, with experience supporting customer-facing or revenue-generating initiatives
Proven ability to analyze and document business requirements, map processes and support delivery through to testing
Confidence working with stakeholders at all levels, with the ability to influence and drive outcomes
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to balance competing priorities and deliver high-quality outputs
Excellent communication, facilitation and stakeholder management skills
A proactive, self-driven approach, with the ability to work autonomously and quickly build context to deliver value from day one

Desirable

Exposure to airline, travel or broader commercial environments

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
A flexible, hybrid work style. Work from home or the office, adapt your location to suit your style and needs
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services
Free counselling and access to a wellbeing app to support your physical, mental, social and financial health
‘Dress for Your Day’ – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you.
One of Brisbane’s most admired head office locations, with South Bank train station, a supermarket, pharmacy, and lunch options downstairs plus a 5-minute walk to the Busway, a rooftop restaurant and onsite gym (with a low membership fee) and South Bank Parklands just across the road

When everyone is included, everyone wins

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQIA+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

About the role
Chariot Corporation Ltd (ASX: CC9) is a lithium exploration company with a growing portfolio of assets across Nigeria and the United States. As we scale rapidly toward our ambition of becoming a leading lithium supplier, we're looking for a Corporate Development Analyst to join our Perth-based team.

You'll work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influencing M&A activity, capital markets strategy, and high-value partnerships — all while building a career with tangible, visible impact in one of the world's most critical supply chains

This is an opportunity to work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influence M&A, capital markets and strategic partnerships—and build a career with real impact.

What you'll be doing

Build financial models and valuation analyses for investments, JV structures, and transactions

Support due diligence across legal, financial, and operational dimensions

Contribute to deal execution, including contract negotiation and project management

Draft ASX announcements, investor materials, and disclosure documents

Support the legal and company secretarial teams on compliance and governance

Stay sharp on market trends, regulatory shifts, and risks shaping the global lithium industry




What we're looking for

Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Law, Engineering (other mining related technical field) with strong academic results

1–5 years' experience in corporate development, investment banking, strategy consulting, or resources

Strong financial modelling and Excel skills—comfortable running valuations and scenario analysis

Strong communication skills—clear, concise, and confident in stakeholder settings

Experience in mining, energy, or professional services is highly regarded

Australian citizenship or permanent residency required

What we offer


Competitive base salary + performance bonuses and equity incentives

Exposure to transformational M&A and growth projects

Hands-on experience across mining, capital markets, and strategy

Opportunities for international travel (USA, West Africa)

A lean, ambitious team where your impact is visible and valued


About us
Chariot Resources Limited is a mineral exploration company focused on discovering and developing high-grade and near surface lithium opportunities focused principally in the United States and Nigeria.




In addition to the recently announced acquisition of a Nigerian lithium portfolio, Chariot has twelve (12) lithium projects in the United States.


Chariot’s USA projects include the Black Mountain Project (which is prospective for hard rock lithium) in Wyoming, USA and the Resurgent Project (which is prospective for claystone lithium) in Nevada and Oregon, USA.


The Nigerian portfolio of hard-rock lithium assets consists of four project clusters (Fonlo, Gbugbu, Iganna, and Saki) in the Oyo and Kwara states which cover approximately 254 square kilometers and are comprised of 8 exploration licences and 2 small-scale mining leases. These assets represent one of the largest portfolios of lithium assets in the country and have a history of significant artisanal lithium mining.

Chariot holds a portfolio interest in certain properties prospective for claystone hosted lithium located in the State of Nevada in the United States through its interest in Mustang Lithium LLC.

Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Release Lead to join a large, complex enterprise environment on a temporary contract to 29 November 2026. This role will lead and coordinate end‑to‑end release planning and execution of the S/4 RISE upgrade, ensuring delivery milestones, cutover activities, and stakeholder expectations are met across integrated enterprise systems.

 

Key highlights:

Strong stakeholder engagement across business and technical teams,
Experience in large ERP environments (SAP S/4HANA, with SAP Payroll highly regarded),

This role suits a confident release professional who can step in quickly, manage competing priorities, and drive outcomes in a complex fast‑paced environment

 

The role: 

Key responsibilities:

Develop project and release planning documentation to support delivery objectives
Coordinate release schedules, milestones and dependent activities across teams
Manage cutover, implementation and production deployment activities
Engage stakeholders and business areas throughout release delivery
Coordinate upgrade, testing and implementation activities across release phases

 

Are you right for this role?

You will be assessed on your ability to demonstrate the following:

Has extensive experience managing ICT release activities and ERP implementations
Can develop complex release plans and delivery schedules
Has strong knowledge of IT service management and release methodologies
Has strong analytical, strategic thinking and problem-solving skills
Can prepare high-quality reports, briefs and strategic documentation

 

Benefits of working with Corporate Services Division:

By joining Corporate Services Division, you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion 

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions. 

 

To apply for this exciting opportunity, submit your resume and a maximum two-page suitability statement OR cover letter online today.

Your key responsibilities

• Undertake high quality, ethical research, including empirical research into complex sentencing matters, and prepare reports, publications and other information for the Attorney-General, members of the community, the judiciary, government and other stakeholders.
• Develop and lead the Council's research program according to the priorities set by the Council, and provide specialist strategic research advice to the Director, QSAC and the Council.
• Provide high level research advice to the Director, QSAC and the Council on issues relating to sentencing in Queensland and other jurisdictions.
• Prepare complex correspondence and submissions.
• Consult with government and non-government agencies, particularly the judiciary, legal profession, interest groups and members of the community as required, and represent the Council on specialist committees.
• Manage the statistical component of the Council's work.
• Manage the Council's research and statistics team.
• Develop and maintain strong relationships with relevant researchers and institutions, and pro-actively identify research opportunities and partnerships.

Applications to remain current for 12 months.

Job Ad Reference: QLD/685598/26

Closing Date: Thursday, 7th May 2026
